notes: Canid mutation library for breeding simulation. Rarity is gameplay-tuned. stat_modifiers use additive normalized values unless otherwise stated.

mutations.name: Melanism
mutations.description: Darkened coat caused by elevated melanin expression; common in wolves and foxes.

mutations.name: Leucism
mutations.description: Partial loss of pigmentation producing pale or patchy coats, usually with normal eye pigment.

mutations.name: Albinism
mutations.description: Near-complete loss of melanin with pale coat and light eyes; useful as a rare visual mutation.

mutations.name: Dilution
mutations.description: Softens or lightens coat colors, creating blue-gray, cream, or washed tones.

mutations.name: Enhanced Brindle
mutations.description: Strengthened brindle striping suitable for wolfdog and jackal hybrids.

mutations.name: Merle Pattern
mutations.description: Patchy marbling pattern that may create striking coats but can carry health concerns if overstacked.

mutations.name: Piebald
mutations.description: Large white patches with colored sections; common in domesticated lineages.

mutations.name: Deep Sable
mutations.description: Rich dark-tipped coat shading common in wolves and dogs.

mutations.name: Cream Wash
mutations.description: Pale cream coat tone often seen in domestic dogs and desert canids.

mutations.name: Black Mask
mutations.description: Facial dark mask common in jackal-like canids and many domestic breeds.

mutations.name: Giantism
mutations.description: Unusually large body size relative to lineage expectations.

mutations.name: Dwarfism
mutations.description: Reduced body size and proportional shortening; useful for miniature experimental lines.

mutations.name: Long-Legged Morph
mutations.description: Extended limbs and taller stance, evoking maned wolf or slim chase-adapted morphs.

mutations.name: Stocky Build
mutations.description: Heavier torso and shorter overall proportions, often seen in bone-crushing or arctic-adapted lines.

mutations.name: Short Muzzle
mutations.description: Reduced muzzle length creating a compact facial profile.

mutations.name: Elongated Muzzle
mutations.description: Longer facial structure supporting scent tracking and classic wolf/fox silhouettes.

mutations.name: Skull Broadening
mutations.description: Wider cranium with increased jaw leverage, suited to robust predators.

mutations.name: Crusher Jaw
mutations.description: Extreme jaw and skull adaptation resembling bone-crushing extinct canids.

mutations.name: Domed Crest
mutations.description: Raised cranial dome or crest producing an ancestral-looking silhouette.

mutations.name: Gigantic Ears
mutations.description: Oversized ears for thermoregulation and sound localization; ideal for desert foxes.

mutations.name: Rounded Ears
mutations.description: Smaller rounded ears common in arctic and compact canids.

mutations.name: Floppy Ears
mutations.description: Downward ear carriage often associated with domestication in dog-like lines.

mutations.name: Semi-Erect Ears
mutations.description: Intermediate ear carriage useful for crossbred lineages.

mutations.name: Extra Bushy Tail
mutations.description: Plush tail fur improves visual appeal and cold handling.

mutations.name: Bobtail
mutations.description: Shortened tail, often a cosmetic novelty in breeding games.

mutations.name: Curled Tail
mutations.description: Tail curls over the back or in a loose hook, common in domesticated lineages.

mutations.name: Hyper-Social
mutations.description: Unusually strong social tolerance and pack affinity.

mutations.name: Solo-Bonded
mutations.description: Prefers single-handler bonding and reduced group sociality.

mutations.name: Extreme Shyness
mutations.description: Anxiety-prone temperament; may be difficult to manage but useful for niche lines.

mutations.name: Fearless
mutations.description: Bold and risk-tolerant temperament common in high-drive hunters.

mutations.name: Hardiness
mutations.description: General health resilience and lower susceptibility to kennel stress.

mutations.name: Low Immunity
mutations.description: Compromised immune system increasing disease risk and reducing viability.

mutations.name: Hybrid Vigor
mutations.description: Heterosis-like bonus from compatible mixed ancestry; strongest in close Canis crosses.

mutations.name: Hybrid Instability
mutations.description: Common penalty in distant or sandbox-only pairings; indicates developmental noise.

mutations.name: Hybrid Sterility
mutations.description: Offspring may be unable to reproduce. Useful as a hard endpoint for extreme crosses.

mutations.name: Trait Mosaic
mutations.description: Patchwork expression of lineage traits that produces highly individualized hybrids.

mutations.name: Adaptive Blend
mutations.description: Balanced hybrid with slightly improved adaptation to mixed habitats.

mutations.name: Ancient Trait Reversion
mutations.description: Reappearance of a deep ancestral feature from extinct canid lineages.

mutations.name: Bone Crusher Heritage
mutations.description: Borrowed ancient trait pattern that increases skull and jaw robustness.

mutations.name: Paleofur Texture
mutations.description: A rough, historic-looking coat texture that suggests extinct lineage expression.

mutations.name: Massive Frame
mutations.description: Enormously oversized build for stress-test and sandbox breeding scenarios.

mutations.name: Tiny Frame
mutations.description: Extremely compact body plan; good for novelty lines and small-dog aesthetics.

mutations.name: Sprint Chassis
mutations.description: Race-build body optimized for burst speed and chase gameplay.

mutations.name: Arctic Plush Coat
mutations.description: Exceptionally dense winter coat suited to polar and subpolar environments.

mutations.name: Desert Ear Expansion
mutations.description: Oversized ear phenotype for extreme desert adaptation.

mutations.name: Shadow Markings
mutations.description: High-contrast dark markings that create a dramatic outline in the kennel viewer.

mutations.name: Warbark Vocalization
mutations.description: Aggressive bark-howl hybrid useful for experimental behavioral lines.

mutations.name: Hyper Fertility
mutations.description: Extremely prolific line trait that increases reproductive output at a slight stability cost.

mutations.name: Hypofertility
mutations.description: Reduced reproductive output; common in unstable hybrids and ancient crosses.

mutations.name: Litter Boost
mutations.description: Improves average litter size without substantially altering viability.

mutations.name: Singleton Bias
mutations.description: Skews pregnancies toward very small litters or single offspring.
